steps.title: Read the logical relationship
steps.content: Sentence 1 says the shell appears external, protecting the body like armor. Sentence 2 says the shell is often incorrectly assumed to be an exoskeleton. Because the shell looks external, people draw the wrong conclusion that it is an exoskeleton.

steps.title: Pick the transition type
steps.content: The first sentence is the cause and the second is the effect (the resulting misconception). A causal connector like 'hence' (meaning 'therefore') is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate by choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — That being said,: signals a concession or pivot, but sentence 2 follows from sentence 1 rather than pushing back against it. ✗
  • Choice B — However,: signals contrast, but the two sentences agree (appearance leads to assumption). ✗
  • Choice C — For instance,: would mark sentence 2 as an example of sentence 1, but the misconception is a consequence, not an instance, of the shell's appearance. ✗
  • Choice D — Hence,: correctly marks the misconception as the result of the shell's external appearance. ✓
That is the correct answer.
